channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Top of langley road croydon road end opposite house number 5, 6th March
item.description:
Rubbish once again been dumped. This is happening every week now. This was dumped between the times 9oclock and 1pm today, daylight. This needs to ne looked into by yourselves not just clearing it up everytime. Believe it the rubbish from either the shops or the flats above.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Langley Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead rat beside the building on Oakfiels Road, 6th March
item.description:
124 Oakfield Rd, London SE20 8RQ Around this address, basically side of One Stop shop has become what seems like a dumping ground for their rubbish/crates and generally always looking dirty and dangerous for children to walk past. Since there is a primary school just along the road, many children walk past the road and it seems generally unsafe and horrible.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oakfield Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole centre of road, 6th March
item.description:
Pothole that has been present for a number of months. Risk to road users including cyclists as there is a bike box on This road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Couthurst Road, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bins outside of 17 Erindale Terrace. SE18 2QG, 6th March
item.description:
Dear sir / Ma, I am a support officer working at 17 Erindale Terrace, which is a supported living service within the community. Can I please bring to you attention a case of three green waste bins lying outside of 17 Erindale terrace, Plumstead. SE18 2QG and being used by passers-by as a community bin. The bins were left outside the property over a year ago when the council changed our bins to the black ones. These bins, we were told back then would be collected by the council at a later date, but it has been over 2 years now that they have been left outside the property. It is both an environmental hazard as well as a hinderance to people walking along the pavement. Please could something be done about coming to remove them from the property, please. Kind regards, Kassim Adewale

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Erindale Terrace, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 20 inch pothole intersection of Dickson road and Whinyates road, 6th March
item.description:
This has been there for nearly 3 months and has been getting in larger in depth.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Whinyates Road, Eltham

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass verge needs cutting back, 6th March
item.description:
I am once again contacting you to raise a concern regarding the lack of maintenance of the grass verge opposite our property. It is becoming increasingly difficult to park there without risking damage to vehicles, as brambles from the adjacent land (formerly a horse field) have become significantly overgrown and now extend onto the grass verge. As a result, it is almost impossible to park without the side of a car being scratched. In addition, people have begun dumping large items such as plastic packing boxes into the brambles. This not only makes the area unsightly but also creates a potential health hazard. Unfortunately, these items are too far into the brambles for me to safely remove them myself. I have previously reported this issue and was advised that the land is not the council’s responsibility and is instead owned by Berkeley Homes. However, from the photographs I took this morning — and particularly from an older photograph from approximately 15 years ago that I located today — it is clear that there is a fence marking the boundary of the field. This indicates that the grass verge between that fence and the road falls within the council’s responsibility. I would therefore be grateful if the council could arrange for its contractors to cut back the brambles, mow the grass verge, remove the dumped items, and ensure the area is properly maintained going forward. Thank you for your attention to this matter

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wallhouse Road, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree fallen down, 6th March
item.description:
A car hit the tree and knocked it over, the tree now blocks the pavement and blocking the path to walk past. It also overhangs peoples driveways. This happened this morning (6/3)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 9, Annandale Road, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Austen Close, Thamesmead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Back garden of 171 Royal college street NW1 0SG, 6th March
item.description:
Tree has over grown, branches hitting windows when windy, breaking fencing, causing damage to neighbours garden path, blocks natural light . This has been ongoing for a few years, I have reported tjis3 years ago but you had no record of it it was also reported last July and I was told they would come and cut all the branches and look into mending the fencing for myself and the neighbours garden. I am getting continued issues from my neighbours about the tree causing damage and this is causing me a high amount of stress. I had a stroke a coupon of years ago and was unable to tend the garden (the tree) and I can’t cope with the way the tree has over taken my outside space causing Camden’s property a lot of damage . Please can this be looked into as soon as possible as it’s taking a toll on my heath now .
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lyme Street, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Please clean Brook Drive, 6th March
item.description:
The waste from uncollected Serco bags and general waste which has fallen out of the Brook Drive bin is all along the pavement and curb. Lambeth needs to clean properly and regularly - not leave it to residents and Southwark residents. We are all fed up with it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 119D, Brook Drive, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
